certProducts.pageSubtitle: Agarwood products inspected and certified by the Vietnam Agarwood Association
certProducts.heroTitle2: has been reviewed by an evaluation council of
certProducts.heroSub: Not self-declared. Not a trend label. This is a {validity}-year certification reviewed by the Association's expert council — and you can put your own product on this list.
certProducts.processSub: Every product shown on this page has completed all 4 steps below — no exceptions, no shortcuts.
certProducts.step1Desc: The company submits the product file, declared price and origin documentation.
certProducts.step2Desc: {count} council members are randomly assigned — multidisciplinary, no overlap with the applicant company.
certProducts.step3Desc: Each member evaluates independently and votes APPROVE or REJECT with detailed remarks.
certProducts.step4Desc: 5/{count} APPROVE → an official seal and verification code are issued, valid for {validity} years.
certProducts.vetoNotice: Veto right: a single REJECT vote rejects the application. Standards are kept strict so the certification carries real weight.
certProducts.noMatchDesc: Try changing the filters or search keywords
certProducts.aspirationDesc: The certification process is open. No big company required — just a product that meets the council's standards.
certProducts.aspirationNote: 100% review fee refund if the application is rejected.
certProducts.toggleConfirmOff: Remove Featured? It will no longer appear in the Featured Products section on the homepage.

landing.finalCtaTitle: Ready to take your brand to the next level?

register.metaDesc: Apply to join the Vietnam Agarwood Association — a B2B community connecting agarwood businesses nationwide.
register.heroDesc: Join the largest agarwood business community in Vietnam — connect, share and grow together.
register.b1Desc: Share experiences and market information on the specialized community feed
register.b2Desc: Products receive an official certification badge — boosting credibility with customers
register.b3Desc: Professional company page on the Association website, SEO optimized
register.b4Desc: Connect with 100+ agarwood businesses for collaboration opportunities
register.s1Desc: Fill out the form with personal or business information
register.s2Desc: Account activated immediately — share posts for free
register.s3Desc: Pay membership fee for higher post limits and homepage priority
register.s4Desc: Product certification, banner advertising, media services
register.approvalP1: Per the Association's Charter, applications are reviewed by the Standing Committee at quarterly meetings; the President issues recognition within 30 days of receiving the complete dossier.
register.approvalP2: Before official recognition, you receive a Basic account so you can join the community and post immediately. After signing in, you may submit the official membership application for the Standing Committee to review.
register.approvalP3: See the full member rights & obligations in the <charterLink>Association Charter (Articles 8–10)</charterLink>.

services.metaDesc: Professional article writing, press release and social media content services for agarwood businesses.
services.heroDesc: Professional media solutions for agarwood businesses — elevate your brand and reach target customers effectively.
services.svc1Desc: Comprehensive introduction of your business, history, products and core values. SEO-optimized, published on reputable channels.
services.svc2Desc: Detailed description of agarwood products, value, origin and quality certifications. Effectively attract potential customers.
services.svc3Desc: Draft and distribute professional press releases to major media channels in the industry.
services.svc4Desc: Produce engaging content for Facebook, Instagram, Zalo OA — boost engagement and brand recognition.
services.step1Desc: Fill out the service order form with details about your media needs.
services.step2Desc: Our team contacts you within 24 hours to confirm requirements and send a detailed quote.
services.step3Desc: Media specialists produce content as requested, with regular progress updates.
services.step4Desc: Deliver completed content with revision support until you are satisfied. Payment after acceptance.
services.orderDesc: Fill in the information below and we will respond within 24 business hours.

about.metaDesc: Learn about the history, mission, vision and leadership of the Vietnam Agarwood Association.
about.heroSub: A professional social organization connecting, preserving, and elevating the value of Vietnamese Agarwood — a thousand-year heritage.
about.introTitle: Connecting Excellence, Preserving Heritage, Elevating Value
about.introLead1: The Vietnam Agarwood Association (VAWA) was established on January 11, 2010, under Decision No. 23/QD-BNV of the Ministry of Home Affairs. It is a voluntary, non-profit professional social organization operating under the Association Charter and Vietnamese law.
about.introLead2: The Association's mission is to connect the community of businesses, artisans, and researchers both domestically and internationally; preserve precious Aquilaria genetic resources and traditional knowledge; and elevate the Vietnamese Agarwood brand in the international arena.
about.introQuote: Vietnamese Agarwood — not just a material, but a thousand-year cultural heritage that needs to be preserved and shared.
about.membersLead: {count} VIP members · businesses · artisans from across Vietnam
about.ctaTitle: Connect, Preserve and Elevate Vietnamese Agarwood Together
about.ctaDesc: Become a member to join Vietnam's leading agarwood business community — connect with experts, participate in events, and promote trade.
about.membersSubtitle: {count} official members journeying with the Association
about.orgChartSubtitle: According to Article 12 of the Association Charter (amended) 2023
about.contactAddress: 150 Ly Chinh Thang Street, Xuan Hoa Ward, Ho Chi Minh City
about.noLeaders: Leadership information is not available yet.
about.noVipMembers: No VIP members are currently shown.

officialChannels.title: Official Communication Channels & Anti-Impersonation Warning
officialChannels.intro: Vietnam Agarwood Association (VAWA) — established under Decision No. 23/QĐ-BNV dated January 11, 2010 of the Ministry of Home Affairs — only communicates through the channels listed below. Any Facebook, Zalo, website, or account impersonating the Association does not belong to us.
officialChannels.notResponsibleTitle: The Association is NOT responsible for:
officialChannels.notResponsibleItem1: Any Facebook page, fanpage, group, Zalo OA, Zalo group, website, TikTok, or YouTube account not on the official channels list above.
officialChannels.notResponsibleItem2: Any transactions, payments, donations, membership fees… made through impersonating accounts or pages.
officialChannels.notResponsibleItem3: Any content, products, or services published in the Association's name by third parties without written approval from the Executive Board.
officialChannels.reportTitle: If you detect an impersonating page/account, please:
officialChannels.reportStep1: Send the URL of the impersonating page to the Association's official email for the Communications Board to handle.
officialChannels.reportStep2: Report the page directly to Facebook / Zalo / Google.
officialChannels.reportStep3: Do not transfer money, provide personal information, or transact with unverified pages.

memberDetail.noProducts: Member hasn't listed any products yet.

printCard.title: Print Member Card
printCard.instructions: The card is rendered at CR80 size (85.6 × 54mm). Press "Print" → choose your printer with landscape A4 paper or specialty ID card stock. The two sides print on two consecutive pages — select "Two-sided" in the dialog to align correctly.
printCard.guide1: ID card paper 85.6×54mm (PVC 0.76mm recommended for durability)
printCard.guide2: Dedicated card printers: Zebra ZC, Fargo DTC, Evolis Zenius — or print on A4 and trim
printCard.guide3: Print at a card bureau: send this file as PDF (Ctrl+P → Save as PDF)
printCard.establishedText: Established under Decision No. 23/QĐ-BNV dated January 11, 2010 of the Ministry of Home Affairs.
